attempts.feedback_to_next_iteration:
[PREVIOUS FINAL ANSWER]

analysisWe need to produce a plan achieving goals: communicated soil data from waypoint3, rock data from waypoint1, image data of objective0 high_res.

We have two rovers: rover0 and rover1.

Initial energies: both 50, enough.

Rover0: at waypoint3, equipped for soil analysis and imaging, stores empty, cameras camera1 (supports colour low_res) and camera2 (supports low_res). camera1 calibrated target objective0, camera2 target objective1. Both on board rover0. No calibration yet.

Rover1: at waypoint2, equipped for soil, rock, imaging, store empty, camera0 on board (supports colour high_res), calibration target objective0. Good for high_res image.

Goal: soil data from waypoint3 (soil sample at waypoint3). There is at_soil_sample waypoint3 initially. So need a rover with soil analysis equipment, empty store, at waypoint3, sample soil, then communicate soil data to lander (general) at waypoint2, with visibility between rover location and lander location (visible ?x ?y). Need channel free (true). Need rover available (available). After communication, rover must be available again (effect sets available). Also need rock data from waypoint1 (rock sample at waypoint1). Need rover with rock analysis equipment, store empty, at waypoint1, sample rock, then communicate rock data.

Rover1 has rock analysis equipment. Rover0 does not. So rock sample must be done by rover1.

Rover1 currently at waypoint2. Need to go to waypoint1 (rock sample). There is can_traverse waypoint2 waypoint1 (yes). Also visible waypoint2 waypoint1 (yes). So navigate rover1 from waypoint2 to waypoint1 costs 8 energy.

Then sample_rock rover1 store rover1store at waypoint1 (requires empty store, rock sample present, equipped). After sample, store becomes full, have_rock_analysis rover1 waypoint1.

Then need to communicate rock data to lander at waypoint2. Need rover in some waypoint x, lander at waypoint2, visible x to waypoint2. Rover1 currently at waypoint1 after sampling. Is waypoint1 visible
[truncated]
il_data rover0 general waypoint3 waypoint3 waypoint2). That's 5 arguments after rover0 and general? Actually we have rover0, general, waypoint3, waypoint3, waypoint2. That's 5 total after the action name: rover0, general, waypoint3, waypoint3, waypoint2. Good.

Similarly communicate_rock_data parameters: (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint). So also 5 arguments: rover, lander, p (rock sample waypoint), x (rover location), y (lander location). So (communicate_rock_data rover1 general waypoint1 waypoint1 waypoint2).

communicate_image_data parameters: (?r - rover ?l - lander ?o - objective ?m - mode ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint). That's 5 arguments after action name: rover, lander, objective, mode, x, y. So (communicate_image_data rover1 general objective0 high_res waypoint1 waypoint2).

Now calibrate parameters: (?r - rover ?i - camera ?t - objective ?w - waypoint). So (calibrate rover1 camera0 objective0 waypoint1).

take_image parameters: (?r - rover ?p - waypoint ?o - objective ?i - camera ?m - mode). So (take_image rover1 waypoint1 objective0 camera0 high_res).

navigate parameters: (?x - rover ?y - waypoint ?z - waypoint). So (navigate rover1 waypoint2 waypoint1).

sample_rock parameters: (?x - rover ?s - store ?p - waypoint). So (sample_rock rover1 rover1store waypoint1).

sample_soil already.

Now ensure that after sample soil, rover0 store full, but not needed.

All good.

Now produce final answer lines in order.

Potential need to recharge? Not needed.

Thus final answer.assistantfinal(sample_soil rover0 rover0store waypoint3)
